calibration.coverage_strategy_note: Real test-takers report this exam covers breadth over depth. Stay at college 101 level on each topic.

subtopic_struggle_signals.note: My school has no SAT prep, but that is not an excuse.
Lets say for instance, there is a equation: 4 + 5x = 192 - 9x.
isn't depth under the water supposed to be negative?

perceived_authenticity_bar.note: Students compare us to these — match the register.

generator_prompt_injection:
REAL TEST-TAKER INSIGHTS (sourced from r/Sat, updated 2026-05-31):
- Coverage: Real test-takers report this exam covers breadth over depth. Stay at college 101 level on each topic.
- High-emphasis topics (per real students): algebra.
- Students struggle most with: sat prep, equation solving, depth under water. Generate proportionally more on these.
- Wording style: ~12% of items should use realistic exam-style indirection (NOT unfair ambiguity).
- Difficulty target: 15% easy / 50% medium / 35% hard. avg pass score 1288 — harder test.
- Authenticity bar: students compare us to Desmos, SAT class, practice tests. Match that register.

popup_tips.text: On SAT MATH, real test-takers say breadth beats depth. Aim to recognize many topics at a 101 level rather than mastering a few in deep detail.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/Sat

popup_tips.text: Where SAT MATH students reported struggling most: sat prep, equation solving, depth under water. Make sure you can handle these before exam day.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/Sat

popup_tips.text: Resources real SAT MATH test-takers credited: Desmos and SAT class. Pair with practice questions.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/Sat

popup_tips.text: On SAT MATH, watch for tricky wording — EXCEPT/NOT-style questions and embedded distractors are common. Read each stem twice.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/Sat
